patches.find: plus an ellipse axis ratio (WIN-030) — four free parameters tuned against 19 city pairs
patches.replace: plus the equatorial ring radius r_eq = 14,105 km introduced in V13 two-zone topology — four free parameters tuned against 19 city pairs
patches.rationale: Curmudgeon c4: WIN-027 and WIN-057 enumerate different fourth parameters. WIN-057 correctly lists r_eq = 14,105 km; WIN-027 incorrectly lists ellipse axis ratio (which is a geometric parameter, not a distance-computation parameter). Align WIN-027 to match WIN-057's enumeration.

patches.find: The Sydney–Santiago corridor (Qantas QF27/QF28) covers a great-circle distance of 11,338 km in about 12 h 40 min nonstop at subsonic cruise speed. On a flat disc, Sydney sits on the ~20,015 km southern rim and Santiago on the opposing rim; the straight-line disc distance exceeds ~30,000 km, almost three times the globe distance.
patches.replace: Direct Sydney–Santiago services (operated nonstop by Qantas at various points, and via Auckland at others) complete ~11,338 km in roughly 13 h of air time. On the flat disc, Sydney (~34°S) and Santiago (~33.5°S) sit near the southern edge at r ≈ 16,000 km from centre, separated by ~138° of azimuth; the planar chord between them exceeds ~30,000 km, almost three times the globe great-circle distance.
patches.rationale: Two c4 holes combined: (1) ISS-1037 QF27/28 nonstop framing — Qantas has operated nonstop and via Auckland; (2) ISS-1044 geometric error — flat disc has only one outer rim at 20,015 km; both cities sit at r ≈ 16,000 km on the same southern arc, not on 'opposing rims'.

patches.find: achieves sub-millimeter absolute accuracy on WGS84 (sub-millimetre absolute error on the full WGS84 ellipsoid, per Karney 2013 — accuracy does not degrade with distance) using exactly two parameters — semi-major axis 6,378,137 m and flattening 1/298.257223563 — and zero latitude-dependent corrections anywhere on Earth.
patches.replace: delivers sub-millimetre absolute accuracy on the WGS84 ellipsoid — error does not accumulate with distance — using exactly two parameters (semi-major axis 6,378,137 m and flattening 1/298.257223563) and zero latitude-dependent corrections anywhere on Earth.
patches.rationale: ISS-1045: Karney/Vincenty sentence double-states the accuracy claim and uses inconsistent American/British spelling. Consolidate to one clause with British spelling (sub-millimetre).

patches.find: 21% unexplained error is substantial; spherical geodesy achieves <0.5% error.
patches.replace: R² 0.79 = 21% unexplained; spherical geodesy achieves sub-millimetre absolute accuracy globally with two parameters.
patches.rationale: ISS-1046: detail_verdict_text opening clashes with detail_evidence that now cites sub-millimetre absolute accuracy. <0.5% error is a weaker statement; sub-millimetre is more accurate and consistent with detail_evidence.

patches.find: the correction exists because the Earth is curved, not because it is flat.
patches.replace: the correction exists because any sphere-to-plane projection produces exactly this distortion pattern — the dome rediscovered the curvature it denies.
patches.rationale: ISS-1038: Opening circular-sounding phrasing 'correction exists because the Earth is curved, not because it is flat' can be dismissed as tautological. Rephrase to make the irony sharper and more informative.

patches.find: Independent of the scientific critique above, this claim also fails our post-hoc test. The numerical value is one the dome framework derives from its own geometric or symbolic parameters (disc radius, firmament height, chosen symmetry axis, or similar), and the "confirmation" consists of matching that derivation to a globe-measured value — which, because the dome parameters were themselves fit to globe data (see Kill-Shot #1 and Part 2b), is circular. There is no pre-registration of the derivation in a venue that timestamps independently of the author, and the dome monitor script (where one exists) encodes the expected value in the same commit as the comparison. Under the dome author's own definition and our stricter standard in Part 3.1b, this is retrodiction, not prediction.
patches.replace: See our post-hoc standard (Part 3.1b) for why all fitted-parameter claims fail the prediction test regardless of the scientific argument above.
patches.rationale: ISS-1039: Post-hoc boilerplate paragraph duplicates what detail_evidence already says more specifically (monitor.py hardcodes, post-hoc adjustment history). Trim to one anchor-link sentence.

patches.find: The SAA covers only a fraction of the latitude band.
patches.replace: The SAA covers only a fraction of the longitude band — about 130 degrees, while the winds encircle the entire Southern Hemisphere.
patches.rationale: ISS-1047: Major factual error — the whole argument is about longitude mismatch, not latitude. SAA covers ~130° of longitude (one-third of circumference) while Roaring 40s exist at all longitudes.

patches.find: the SAA occupies only about 120° of longitude
patches.replace: the SAA occupies only about 130° of longitude
patches.rationale: ISS-1049: tldr_evidence says 120° but detail_evidence cites 90°W–40°E = 130°, and detail_verdict_text also says 130°. Align tldr_evidence to match.

patches.find: Only Domain 17 (AAO index) accesses live NOAA data, but it tests only whether the current atmospheric oscillation index is elevated, not whether the Roaring 40s are caused by the SAA.
patches.replace: Only Domain 17 (AAO index) accesses live NOAA data, but it tests only whether the current atmospheric oscillation index is elevated, not whether the Roaring 40s are caused by the SAA — and the conditional design (AAO > 0.3 sigma WHEN SAA decay > 50 nT/yr) passes trivially in any month when the second clause is false, so the test is permissive of independent atmospheric variation.
patches.rationale: ISS-1050: Domain 17 sentence does not explain why the test is permissive. Adding the conditional design detail makes explicit that Domain 17 is easy to pass and cannot verify the causal claim.

ISS-1048: code_analysis.monitoring is live_fetch but two of three domains (27 and 33) hardcode pass=True. Only Domain 17 has live data access. The win is predominantly hardcoded. Flip to hardcoded to match the evidence in detail_evidence.
